staticText: Use current official GEPF guidance and keep benefit boundaries explicit. The public AfroTools route estimates retirement only for members with at least 10 years of vested service, where Government Gazette 51202 provides an unambiguous formula. Ask for final annual pensionable salary plus vested, remaining savings and retirement service records, and whether the member is an other or services member. Vested gratuity is 6.72% of final annual salary times vested service. Savings gratuity is three times 7.75% for other members or three times 8.45% for services members, multiplied by final annual salary and savings service. Annual annuity is final annual salary times vested service divided by 55, plus 1.5 times final annual salary times retirement service divided by 58.65 for other members or 60.71 for services members, plus R360. For standard early retirement before age 60, reduce retirement amounts by one-third of one percent per month; employer-authorised early retirement may differ and needs confirmation. Members contribute 7.5% of pensionable salary. Employers contribute 13% for other members or 16% for services members. Do not estimate another vested-service profile, resignation, retrenchment, savings withdrawal, actuarial interest, tax, home-loan eligibility, GEMS, UIF, survivor amounts, enhanced spouse options, investment growth, pension increases or private-fund drawdown. The revised actuarial interest factors took effect on 1 October 2025, so never recreate a withdrawal value from contribution growth. Route users to GEPF for a formal estimate and current service statement. State that AfroTools is a local planning estimate, not GEPF, SARS or financial advice. Official sources reviewed 23 July 2026: Government Gazette 51202, GEPF retirement benefits, employer contributions and the 1 October 2025 actuarial-factor clarification.
